majors.count_method: counted unique program pages linked from the official Caltech 'Majors & Minors' page
majors.notes: counted unique program pages linked from the official Caltech 'Majors & Minors' page; titles extracted from official page: https://www.admissions.caltech.edu/why-caltech/academics/majors-minors

admissions.application_platform: Caltech accepts the Common App or QuestBridge Application for first-year applicants.
admissions.testing_policy: Caltech requires first-year applicants to submit either the SAT or the ACT. If applicants have taken AP or IB exams, Caltech also asks them to submit those scores.
admissions.gpa_policy: Caltech does not publish a minimum GPA. Instead, it requires official secondary-school transcripts for grades 9-11, a school report, and a mid-year senior report when available.
admissions.course_rigor: Caltech expects mastery in four years of math including calculus, one year each of physics and chemistry, one year of biology recommended, four years of English, and two years of history and/or social sciences with three or more years recommended. If calculus, chemistry, or physics is unavailable because of an unresolvable conflict, Caltech allows specific AP, IB, or Schoolhouse.world substitutes with counselor documentation.
admissions.recommendations: Caltech requires two teacher or instructor recommendations: one from STEM (calculus or beyond, chemistry, physics, or biology) and one from humanities or social sciences.
admissions.essays: Caltech requires supplemental essays alongside the application, including a required STEM academic-interest question, one STEM experience prompt, a creativity-in-action response, and two short-answer responses chosen from four prompts.

projects_research: Caltech welcomes optional supplemental research papers or publications, with a citation if published plus a recommendation letter and research description.

evidence.source_excerpt: A phase-4 count of unique major/minor program pages linked from Caltech's official 'Majors & Minors' page yielded 31 programs.

evidence.claim: Caltech requires first-year applicants to submit either the SAT or the ACT, and applicants who have taken AP or IB exams are asked to submit those scores as well.
evidence.source_excerpt: Standardized Tests Score: We require either the SAT or ACT test scores. If you have taken AP or IB examinations, you will be asked to submit those scores as well.

evidence.claim: Caltech expects mastery in calculus, physics, chemistry, English, and history/social sciences, with specific substitute pathways when core courses are unavailable.
evidence.source_excerpt: To prepare for core, students must demonstrate mastery in the following subjects: Four years of math, including one year of calculus ... One year of physics ... One year of chemistry ... If a student is unable to take a calculus, chemistry, and/or physics course in high school ... Caltech will accept examination scores or the course challenge through Schoolhouse.world ...

evidence.claim: Caltech requires two teacher or instructor recommendations: one STEM and one humanities or social sciences.
evidence.source_excerpt: Letters of Recommendation: 1 STEM: Calculus (or beyond), Chemistry, Physics, or Biology; 1 Humanities or Social Sciences: English, History, Government, or Economics.

evidence.claim: Caltech requires supplemental essays including a STEM academic-interest question, one STEM experience prompt, a creativity-in-action response, and two short-answer responses chosen from four prompts.
evidence.source_excerpt: Required STEM Academic Interest Question ... Select one of the following two STEM Experience prompts ... Creativity in Action Question ... Choose two of the four questions below and answer both.

evidence.claim: Caltech explicitly welcomes optional supplemental research materials, including papers or publications.
evidence.source_excerpt: Supplemental Materials ... Research paper or publication preferred (citation if published, letter of recommendation, and research description required).

Aerospace; Applied Physics; Astrophysics; Bioengineering; Biology; Chemical Engineering; Chemistry; Computational and Neural Systems; Computer Science; Control and Dynamical Systems; Economics; Electrical Engineering

evidence.claim: Ranked top 20 professors from RateMyProfessors by rating count and sentiment metrics.
evidence.source_excerpt: Matched RateMyProfessors school 'California Institute of Technology' (legacyId=148) and ranked up to 20 professors with at least one rating.

evidence.claim: Ranked top 20 notable alumni from Wikipedia-derived candidate lists using trailing pageviews.
evidence.source_excerpt: Candidates were harvested from school-specific Wikipedia alumni/people pages or categories, filtered to likely people, and ranked by pageviews. Major-specific and last-10-years filtering requires stronger school-specific alumni sources than this generic pipeline currently has.

evidence.claim: Caltech accepts the Common App or QuestBridge Application for first-year applicants.
evidence.source_excerpt: Application materials: Common App or QuestBridge Application.

evidence.claim: Caltech does not publish a minimum GPA; it instead requires official secondary-school transcripts, a school report, and a mid-year report.
evidence.source_excerpt: Official transcripts from your secondary school (grades 9-11 ...). Mid-Year Report (due when first term senior grades are available at your school).

warnings: Major titles extracted from official school source (33 titles).
Phase 4 structured upgrade applied to top-10 expansion schools.
School-people enrichment uses public third-party sources (RateMyProfessors and Wikipedia/Wikimedia), so rankings are heuristic rather than official university data.

popular_professors.ranking_basis: RateMyProfessors entries sorted by rating_count desc, then average_rating desc, then would_take_again_percent desc.
popular_professors.source_summary: Matched RateMyProfessors school 'California Institute of Technology' (legacyId=148) and ranked up to 20 professors with at least one rating.

successful_alumni.ranking_basis: Curated from official Caltech Alumni Distinguished Alumni Award profiles and official Caltech Alumni Techer stories/news pages, prioritizing person-specific profiles with explicit degree-year evidence and strong evidence of post-graduation impact.
successful_alumni.source_summary: Extracted from official Caltech Alumni pages on alumni.caltech.edu, primarily Distinguished Alumni Award profiles plus recent Techer stories and news features.

items.name: Fei-Fei Li
items.bio: Caltech Alumni says Fei-Fei Li (MS '02, PhD '05) chose Caltech for its interdisciplinarity and is helping guide AI technology toward broad human benefit as a leading computer vision and artificial intelligence researcher.
items.description: AI leader and computer vision researcher.
items.confirmation_label: Official Caltech Alumni Distinguished Alumni Award profile
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Caltech Alumni person-specific award profile with explicit degree-year evidence and substantive career summary.

items.name: Bob Behnken
items.bio: Caltech Alumni says NASA astronaut Bob Behnken (MS '93, PhD '97) was honored for an accomplished career across three space missions, including the first crewed orbital flight aboard a commercially developed spacecraft in 2020.
items.confirmation_label: Official Caltech Alumni Distinguished Alumni Award profile
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Caltech Alumni person-specific award profile with explicit degree-year evidence and substantive career summary.

items.name: David Brin
items.bio: Caltech Alumni says David Brin (BS '73) was recognized for enduring excellence in storytelling and for examining how change, science, and technology affect the human condition in his New York Times-bestselling science fiction novels.
items.confirmation_label: Official Caltech Alumni Distinguished Alumni Award profile
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Caltech Alumni person-specific award profile with explicit degree-year evidence and substantive career summary.

items.name: Eugene Myers
items.bio: Caltech Alumni says Eugene Myers (BS '75), director of the Max Planck Institute of Molecular Cell Biology and Genetics, made a transformative impact on bioinformatics by creating the basic local alignment search tool and helping drive genome analysis.
items.confirmation_label: Official Caltech Alumni Distinguished Alumni Award profile
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Caltech Alumni person-specific award profile with explicit degree-year evidence and substantive career summary.

items.name: Laurie Leshin
items.bio: Caltech Alumni says Laurie Leshin (MS '89, PhD '95), president of Worcester Polytechnic Institute, was lauded for barrier-breaking leadership at WPI and previous leadership roles at NASA and Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute.
items.description: University president and former NASA leader.
items.confirmation_label: Official Caltech Alumni Distinguished Alumni Award profile
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Caltech Alumni person-specific award profile with explicit degree-year evidence and substantive career summary.

items.name: Charles Elachi
items.bio: Caltech Alumni says Charles Elachi (MS '69, PhD '71), Caltech professor emeritus and former JPL director, was recognized for distinguished leadership in space exploration and planetary science.
items.description: Former JPL director and planetary scientist.
items.confirmation_label: Official Caltech Alumni Distinguished Alumni Award profile
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Caltech Alumni person-specific award profile with explicit degree-year evidence and substantive career summary.

items.name: Louise Chow
items.bio: Caltech Alumni says Louise Chow (PhD '73) developed pioneering electron microscopy methods that enabled the Nobel Prize-winning discovery of split genes, one of molecular biology's landmark breakthroughs.
items.description: Molecular biologist tied to the discovery of split genes.
items.confirmation_label: Official Caltech Alumni Distinguished Alumni Award profile
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Caltech Alumni person-specific award profile with explicit degree-year evidence and substantive career summary.

items.name: Nader Engheta
items.bio: Caltech Alumni says Nader Engheta (MS '79, PhD '82), the H. Nedwill Ramsey Professor at the University of Pennsylvania, is recognized for pioneering advances in optics including optical nanocircuits and metamaterials.
items.confirmation_label: Official Caltech Alumni Distinguished Alumni Award profile
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Caltech Alumni person-specific award profile with explicit degree-year evidence and substantive career summary.

items.bio: Caltech Alumni says Timothy M. Swager (PhD '88) earned recognition for groundbreaking advances in molecular electronics and chemical sensing that produced a new generation of highly sensitive devices.
items.description: Chemist known for molecular electronics and sensing.

items.evidence_quality_note: Official Caltech Alumni person-specific award profile with explicit degree-year evidence and substantive career summary.

items.bio: Caltech Alumni says Bette Korber (PhD '88), a laboratory fellow at Los Alamos National Laboratory, is lauded for pioneering virology research and computational vaccine design, especially in HIV science.

items.evidence_quality_note: Official Caltech Alumni person-specific award profile with explicit degree-year evidence and substantive career summary.

items.bio: Caltech Alumni says Karen Maples (BS '76) served as an obstetrician and gynecologist at Kaiser Permanente for 38 years and was recognized as one of Caltech's first Black female undergraduates and a lifelong mentor and advocate.

items.bio: Caltech Alumni says Bill Gross (BS '81) is building a new search startup aimed at protecting creators in the age of AI after earlier helping shape the modern search industry.

items.bio: Caltech Alumni says Tammy Ma (BS '05) directs the Livermore Institute for Fusion Technology at Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory and is trying to make inertial fusion energy real.
items.description: Fusion-energy physicist and national lab leader.

items.bio: Caltech Alumni says Lauren Edgar (MS '09, PhD '13) trained on Mars rover missions, helped plan the first crewed lunar landing for Artemis, and was selected for NASA's 2025 astronaut candidate class.

items.bio: Caltech Alumni says Adam Subhas (PhD '17), an oceanographer at Woods Hole Oceanographic Institution, is leading a first-of-its-kind ocean alkalinity experiment to study carbon capture and acidification.
items.description: Oceanographer leading a novel carbon-removal experiment.
